title: supergateway-rs: Rust Rewrite using Asupersync
description:
Rewrite supergateway (~2,100 LOC TypeScript MCP transport bridge) in Rust using Asupersync.
Drop-in CLI replacement bridging MCP stdio servers with SSE, Streamable HTTP, and WebSocket transports.

6 transport modes: stdioŌåÆSSE, stdioŌåÆWS, stdioŌåÆStateful HTTP, stdioŌåÆStateless HTTP, SSEŌåÆstdio, HTTPŌåÆstdio.

Targets: <3MB RSS idle, <2ms p99 bridge latency, >10k msg/sec throughput, zero leaked children/FDs.

Source: /c/work/supergateway-rs/PRD.md (v6)

title: US-000: Asupersync Web Stack Validation Spike
description:
GO/NO-GO GATE ŌĆö Validate Asupersync can serve the required workloads before committing to the rewrite.

This is a time-boxed spike (1-2 days) to build small proof-of-concept programs exercising every Asupersync capability the project needs. Each item is marked works / works-with-fallback / blocked. If any critical item is blocked with no fallback, the project does not proceed.

## Tech Stack
- Runtime: Asupersync 0.2.7 (NOT Tokio)
- Nightly Rust (pin in rust-toolchain.toml)
- All async operations use `&mut Cx` context

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HTTP server: POST/GET/DELETE routes via `asupersync::web::Router` or `App::builder()`
- [ ] SSE streaming: push events one-at-a-time over long-lived connection. If batch-only, demonstrate raw chunked `text/event-stream`
- [ ] WebSocket: upgrade via `WebSocketUpgrade` extractor or manual HTTP upgrade + `net::websocket`. Bidirectional exchange
- [ ] HTTP client: GET and POST via `asupersync::http` or raw TCP
- [ ] SSE client: connect, receive streamed events
- [ ] Child process: determine if `asupersync::process::Command` exists. If not, demonstrate `std::process::Command` with async I/O adapter inside a Region
- [ ] Verify `kill_on_drop` or equivalent ŌĆö child killed when Region closes
- [ ] Verify `&mut Cx` vs `&Cx` in handler signatures. **Critical:** Determine how concurrent HTTP request handlers each get their own `&mut Cx`. Does the framework fork/split contexts internally? This is a blocking architectural question
- [ ] Timer: `asupersync::time::timeout` wrapping a future
- [ ] Region lifecycle: spawn tasks, cancel region, verify cleanup
- [ ] Sync primitives: `mutex.lock(&cx).await?` works as documented
- [ ] Evaluate `fastmcp_rust` ŌĆö reusable MCP handling? Document findings
- [ ] LabRuntime: deterministic test with virtual time works
- [ ] Nightly Rust: pin toolchain version, verify Docker build
- [ ] **Go/no-go documented.** For each item: works / works-with-fallback / blocked. If any critical item is blocked with no fallback, project does not proceed

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings
notes:
Deliverable: Create spike/FINDINGS.md with a table:
| Item | Status | Notes | Fallback Approach |
Status values: works / works-with-fallback / blocked

Each US-005+ bead references "per US-000 findings" for API availability decisions.
The findings file MUST be at a predictable path for downstream beads to consume.

title: US-009: Signal Handling and Graceful Shutdown
description:
Signal handling for graceful shutdown across all modes. Manages SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP, child cleanup, and PID 1 zombie reaping.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync signal handling (per US-000 findings), else `nix::sys::signal`
- Integration with Region cancellation for cascading cleanup

## File: src/signal.rs

## Signal Handling
- SIGTERM: initiate graceful shutdown
- SIGINT: same as SIGTERM (Ctrl+C)
- SIGHUP: same as SIGTERM (treat as shutdown, not reload)
- On signal received:
  1. Log signal at info level
  2. Cancel top-level Region (cascades to all child Regions ŌåÆ kills children)
  3. Stop accepting new connections
  4. Wait for in-flight requests to drain (up to 5s per US-006 session drain)
  5. Overall shutdown timeout: 10s backstop. If not cleanly shut down after 10s, force exit(1)

## Exit Codes
- 0: clean shutdown (signal received, all children exited)
- 1: error (child spawn failure, bind failure, unhandled error)
- 1: forced shutdown (10s backstop exceeded)

## Stdin EOF Detection (Server Modes)
In server modes (stdioŌåÆSSE/WS/HTTP), detect stdin EOF as a shutdown signal:
- Spawn a task that reads stdin. On EOF, trigger same shutdown as SIGTERM
- This handles the case where the parent process closes stdin (e.g., IDE closes)
- Only in server modes. Client modes already read stdin for MCP messages

## PID 1 Zombie Reaping
When running as PID 1 (Docker container):
- Install SIGCHLD handler with `waitpid(-1, WNOHANG)` loop
- Reap ALL zombie children, not just our direct child
- This is the ONLY place zombie reaping happens ŌĆö ChildBridge (US-005) only reaps its own child
- Check `getpid() == 1` at startup to enable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SIGTERM handler: cancel top-level Region, drain, exit
- [ ] SIGINT handler: same as SIGTERM
- [ ] SIGHUP handler: same as SIGTERM (not reload)
- [ ] 10s overall shutdown backstop: force exit(1) if not clean
- [ ] Stdin EOF detection in server modes: trigger shutdown on EOF
- [ ] Exit code 0 for clean shutdown, 1 for error/forced
- [ ] PID 1 zombie reaping: SIGCHLD handler with waitpid(-1, WNOHANG) loop
- [ ] Child processes killed via Region cancellation (cascades to ChildBridge kill sequence)
- [ ] Integration test: send SIGTERM, verify child killed within 2s
- [ ] Integration test: PID 1 zombie reaping (if testable ŌĆö may need Docker)

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings

title: US-010: Gateway ŌĆö stdio->Streamable HTTP (Stateful)
description:
Gateway mode: stdioŌåÆStreamable HTTP (stateful). Per-session child process, full MCP session lifecycle with Mcp-Session-Id header.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync HTTP server (per US-000 findings)
- Session manager (US-006) for session lifecycle
- ChildBridge (US-005) spawned per session
- GatewayError (US-003a) for error responses
- Codec (US-004) for child stdin/stdout
- Logger + Metrics (US-008) for observability
- Signal handler (US-009) for shutdown

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_stateful_http.rs

## Endpoints
- `POST /mcp` (configurable via --streamableHttpPath): JSON-RPC request/notification
- `GET /mcp`: SSE stream for server-initiated messages
- `DELETE /mcp`: terminate session
- Health endpoints as configured by --healthEndpoint

## Protocol Version Validation
Supported versions: ["2025-06-18", "2025-03-26", "2024-11-05", "2024-10-07"]
Check `protocolVersion` in initialize request params. If not in supported list, return JSON-RPC error -32602 (Invalid params).

## POST Handler
1. Validate Content-Type: application/json (else 415)
2. Validate Accept: application/json, text/event-stream (else 406)
3. Parse JSON body (malformed ŌåÆ 400 + JSON-RPC -32700)
4. If no Mcp-Session-Id header AND message is initialize request: create new session
5. If Mcp-Session-Id header: look up session (not found ŌåÆ 404)
6. Acquire SessionAccessGuard (RAII counter)
7. Write message(s) to child stdin via codec
8. If request (has id): respond with SSE stream, send response events as `event: message\ndata: <json>\n\n`
9. If notification only (no id): return 202 Accepted immediately
10. If batch: write all to child, return SSE stream with responses for all requests in batch

## Batch POST Handling
- JSON body is array ŌåÆ batch of messages
- Forward entire batch to child stdin as single line (re-serialized JSON array)
- Response SSE stream contains one event per response from child
- All responses for the batch's request IDs collected, then stream closed
- Notifications in batch: no response expected for those

## GET Handler (SSE Stream)
1. Validate Accept: text/event-stream (else 406)
2. Require Mcp-Session-Id header (else 400)
3. Look up session (not found ŌåÆ 404)
4. Only ONE GET SSE stream per session (second attempt ŌåÆ 409 Conflict)
5. Return SSE stream with server-initiated notifications/requests from child
6. Keep-alive: send `: keepalive\n\n` comment every 30s

## DELETE Handler
1. Require Mcp-Session-Id header (not found ŌåÆ 404)
2. Transition session to Closing state
3. Return 202 Accepted
4. After session drains (5s max), session transitions to Closed

## Mcp-Session-Id Lifecycle
- Created on initialize response (set in response headers)
- Required on all subsequent requests
- After DELETE: session returns 404 (NOT 503 ŌĆö session no longer exists)
- After session timeout: same as DELETE (404)

## POST Response Correlation
Multiple POSTs may be in-flight for same session concurrently.
Each POST with request id(s) gets its own SSE stream response.
Must correlate child stdout responses to the correct POST's SSE stream by matching JSON-RPC id.
Notifications from child go to GET SSE stream (not POST response streams).

## Child Exit Detection
If child process exits unexpectedly:
- Mark session as failed
- Close GET SSE stream with error event
- Any pending POST SSE streams get JSON-RPC -32603 Internal Error response
- Session transitions to Closed
- Return 502 for subsequent requests to this session

## EPIPE Handling
If writing to child stdin fails with EPIPE:
- Same as child exit detection above
- Mark child dead, fail session, return 502

## Error Response Body Format
- Transport-level errors (404, 406, 409, 415): plain text body
- Application-level errors (400, 502, 503): JSON-RPC error body `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","error":{"code":<code>,"message":"<msg>"},"id":null}`
- Uses GatewayError::into_response() from US-003a

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST creates new session on initialize (returns Mcp-Session-Id header)
- [ ] POST to existing session: validate session ID, acquire access guard, forward to child
- [ ] POST batch: forward batch to child, return SSE stream with all responses
- [ ] POST notification-only: return 202 immediately
- [ ] POST response correlation: match child responses to correct POST SSE stream by JSON-RPC id
- [ ] GET SSE stream: one per session, server-initiated messages, 30s keepalive
- [ ] GET SSE stream conflict: second GET returns 409
- [ ] DELETE: transition to Closing, return 202, drain up to 5s, then Closed
- [ ] Mcp-Session-Id lifecycle: 404 after DELETE (not 503)
- [ ] Protocol version validation: reject unsupported versions with -32602
- [ ] Content-Type validation: 415 for non-application/json POST
- [ ] Accept header validation: 406 for missing required Accept values
- [ ] Child exit detection: mark session failed, close streams, return 502
- [ ] EPIPE handling: same as child exit
- [ ] --streamableHttpPath configurable (default /mcp)
- [ ] Error responses use GatewayError (correct HTTP status + body format)
- [ ] Max sessions enforced (503)
- [ ] Session timeout with access counting (timeout only when count=0)
- [ ] Health endpoint returns 503 until child ready
- [ ] Integration test: full initialize ŌåÆ tool call ŌåÆ response ŌåÆ DELETE lifecycle
- [ ] Integration test: concurrent POSTs to same session
- [ ] Integration test: session timeout fires after idle period

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings
notes:
CRITICAL DETAILS FROM PRD:

Supported Protocol Versions: ["2025-06-18", "2025-03-26", "2024-11-05", "2024-10-07"]

Error Response Body Format:
- Transport-level errors (406, 415, 404, 409): plain text body
- Application-level errors (400, 503): JSON-RPC error object {jsonrpc:"2.0", error:{code, message}, id:null}

Body Parsing: Parse JSON body before handler (express.json() equivalent). Unlike SSE mode which reads raw body.

Concurrent POST Response Correlation:
- Multiple POSTs may be in-flight for same session
- Each POST with id gets its own SSE stream response
- Must correlate child stdout responses to the correct POST's SSE stream by matching JSON-RPC id
- Notifications from child go to GET SSE stream (not POST response streams)

title: US-011: Gateway ŌĆö stdio->Streamable HTTP (Stateless)
description:
Gateway mode: stdioŌåÆStreamable HTTP (stateless). Per-request child process with auto-initialization.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync HTTP server
- ChildBridge (US-005) spawned per request
- Auto-init state machine for transparent child initialization
- GatewayError (US-003a) for error responses
- Codec (US-004) for child stdin/stdout
- Logger + Metrics (US-008), Signal handler (US-009)

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_stateless_http.rs

## Endpoints
- `POST /mcp` (configurable via --streamableHttpPath): JSON-RPC request/notification
- `GET /mcp`: 405 Method Not Allowed (no persistent sessions)
- `DELETE /mcp`: 405 Method Not Allowed
- Health endpoints as configured

## Auto-Init State Machine
For each incoming request:
1. Spawn new ChildBridge
2. Send synthetic initialize request to child (see exact shapes below)
3. Wait for initialize response from child (capture but do NOT return to client)
4. Send `notifications/initialized` to child
5. Now send the actual client request to child
6. Return child's response to client
7. Kill child after response sent

Two separate buffers:
- **Init buffer**: captures initialize response (discarded after auto-init)
- **Request buffer**: captures response to actual client request (returned to client)

## Auto-Init Request Shapes
Initialize request:
```json
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": "init_<timestamp_ms>_<random_alphanumeric_9>",
  "method": "initialize",
  "params": {
    "protocolVersion": "<--protocolVersion flag, default 2024-11-05>",
    "capabilities": { "roots": { "listChanged": true }, "sampling": {} },
    "clientInfo": { "name": "supergateway", "version": "<CARGO_PKG_VERSION>" }
  }
}
```

notifications/initialized:
```json
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "notifications/initialized"
}
```

Use `env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")` for version. Init request id prefix `init_` avoids collision with client numeric ids.

## Batch POST Handling
- Client sends JSON array ŌåÆ batch of messages
- Each message in batch goes through auto-init on same child (init once, then send all)
- Responses collected and returned as SSE stream events
- If batch contains only notifications: return 202

## Error Response Body Format
Same as US-010:
- Transport-level errors (405, 406, 415): plain text
- Application-level errors (400, 500, 502): JSON-RPC error body
- Uses GatewayError from US-003a

## Child Exit During Request
If child exits before sending response:
- Return 502 with JSON-RPC -32603 Internal Error
- Clean up child resources
- Log at error level

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST: spawn child, auto-init, forward request, return response, kill child
- [ ] Auto-init: exact JSON shapes as specified above
- [ ] Auto-init timeout: if child doesn't respond to initialize within 10s, return 502
- [ ] Two separate buffers: init buffer (discarded) and request buffer (returned)
- [ ] POST batch: auto-init once, forward all, collect responses as SSE stream
- [ ] POST notification-only: auto-init, forward, return 202 (no response to collect)
- [ ] GET/DELETE: return 405 Method Not Allowed
- [ ] Content-Type validation: 415 for non-application/json
- [ ] Accept header validation: 406 for missing required values
- [ ] --streamableHttpPath configurable (default /mcp)
- [ ] --protocolVersion used in auto-init params
- [ ] Child exit during request: return 502, clean up
- [ ] Error responses use GatewayError (correct HTTP status + body format)
- [ ] Max concurrent requests enforced (1024 default, same as max sessions)
- [ ] Pending message buffer: Vec<RawMessage> (fixes TS bug D-005 of single-variable overwrite)
- [ ] Health endpoint
- [ ] Integration test: initialize passthrough, tool call, notification
- [ ] Integration test: auto-init timeout
- [ ] Integration test: batch request

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings

title: US-012: Gateway ŌĆö stdio->SSE
description:
Gateway mode: stdioŌåÆSSE. Single shared child process, broadcast to all connected SSE clients.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync HTTP server
- Single ChildBridge (US-005) shared across all clients
- GatewayError (US-003a) for error responses
- Codec (US-004) for child stdin/stdout
- Logger + Metrics (US-008), Signal handler (US-009)

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_sse.rs

## Endpoints
- `GET <ssePath>` (default /sse): SSE event stream. Returns endpoint URL in first event
- `POST <messagePath>` (default /message): JSON-RPC message from client to child
- Health endpoints as configured

## SSE Stream
- First event: `event: endpoint\ndata: <messagePath URL>\n\n`
- Subsequent events: `event: message\ndata: <json-rpc-message>\n\n`
- Keep-alive: `: keepalive\n\n` every 30s
- `X-Accel-Buffering: no` header on SSE response (Nginx proxy compatibility)

## baseUrl Behavior
- `--baseUrl ""` (empty string, default): endpoint URL is relative path (e.g., `/message`)
- `--baseUrl "http://example.com"`: endpoint URL is absolute (e.g., `http://example.com/message`)

## Broadcast Pattern
ALL child stdout messages are broadcast to ALL connected SSE clients (B-001 in DIVERGENCES.md).
This is intentional ŌĆö single shared child, multiple clients see all messages.

## POST Handler
1. Read raw body as string (NOT parsed as JSON by framework ŌĆö raw passthrough)
2. Validate: non-empty body required
3. Malformed JSON: return 400 Bad Request (plain text)
4. Write to child stdin via codec
5. Return 202 Accepted (always, regardless of child processing ŌĆö B-002 in DIVERGENCES.md)

## Stdin Write Serialization
Multiple SSE clients may POST concurrently. All writes to shared child stdin MUST go through a Mutex<ChildStdinWriter> to prevent interleaved JSON lines on the pipe.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GET /sse: returns SSE stream with endpoint event, then message events
- [ ] POST /message: forward to child stdin, return 202
- [ ] Broadcast: all child stdout messages sent to all connected SSE clients
- [ ] X-Accel-Buffering: no header on SSE responses
- [ ] baseUrl: empty string = relative URL, non-empty = absolute URL
- [ ] Malformed JSON POST: return 400 Bad Request (plain text)
- [ ] Stdin write serialization: Mutex prevents interleaved writes from concurrent POSTs
- [ ] SSE keep-alive: 30s comment lines
- [ ] Custom headers applied (from --header, via CORS middleware)
- [ ] Health endpoint returns 503 until child ready
- [ ] Client disconnect: remove from broadcast list, log
- [ ] Integration test: connect SSE, POST message, receive broadcast response

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings

title: US-013: Gateway ŌĆö stdio->WebSocket
description:
Gateway mode: stdioŌåÆWebSocket. Single shared child process, per-client ID routing via HashMap multiplexer.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync WebSocket server (per US-000 findings)
- Single ChildBridge (US-005) shared across all WS clients
- ID multiplexer: HashMap<MangledId, (ClientId, OriginalId)> (fixes TS bug D-001)
- GatewayError (US-003a) for error responses
- Codec (US-004) for child stdin/stdout
- Logger + Metrics (US-008), Signal handler (US-009)

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_ws.rs

## WebSocket Endpoint
- `WS <messagePath>` (default /message): WebSocket upgrade
- Health endpoints as configured (HTTP, not WS)

## ID Multiplexer (Fixes TS Bug D-001)
TS mangles IDs via string concatenation (`clientId + ':' + msg.id`). This breaks string IDs and IDs with colons.
Rust uses HashMap-based routing:
```rust
type MangledId = u64;
type ClientId = u64;
type OriginalId = Box<RawValue>;  // preserved exactly

struct IdMultiplexer {
    next_id: AtomicU64,
    map: RwLock<HashMap<MangledId, (ClientId, OriginalId)>>,
}
```
On clientŌåÆchild: replace message id with auto-increment MangledId, store mapping.
On childŌåÆclient: look up MangledId, restore OriginalId, route to correct client.

## Notification Handling (No ID)
Notifications from child (no id field) are BROADCAST to all clients.
Notifications from client (no id) are forwarded to child without mangling (no id to route back).

## Broadcast Without Head-of-Line Blocking
When broadcasting (notifications from child):
1. Acquire read lock on client map
2. Clone all client channel senders into a local Vec
3. Drop the lock
4. Iterate Vec, try_send to each
NEVER hold RwLock guard across async sends ŌĆö causes deadlocks.

## Backpressure
Per-client bounded channel (256 messages capacity).
- If client is slow (channel full), wait up to 30s for capacity
- If still full after 30s: disconnect that client (WebSocket close frame 1008 Policy Violation)
- Log at warn level when disconnecting slow client

## Stdin Write Serialization
Multiple WS clients send concurrently. All writes to shared child stdin go through Mutex<ChildStdinWriter>.

## EPIPE Handling
If child stdin write fails with EPIPE:
- Close all WebSocket clients with close frame 1011 (Unexpected Condition)
- Log at error level
- If child exits, same behavior

## --header Support
Custom headers (--header) applied to WebSocket upgrade response (fixes TS omission D-105).

## WebSocket Close Frame Codes
- 1000 Normal Closure: clean shutdown
- 1008 Policy Violation: client too slow (backpressure disconnect)
- 1011 Unexpected Condition: child died / EPIPE

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WS endpoint on messagePath, accepts WebSocket upgrade
- [ ] ID multiplexer: HashMap routing, auto-increment MangledId, OriginalId preserved
- [ ] ClientŌåÆchild: mangle id, forward via codec
- [ ] ChildŌåÆclient: unmangle id, route to correct client via multiplexer
- [ ] Notifications (no id): broadcast to ALL clients
- [ ] Broadcast: clone senders, drop lock, then send (no HOL blocking)
- [ ] Backpressure: 256 msg bounded channel, 30s timeout, disconnect slow clients
- [ ] WS close frames: 1000 (normal), 1008 (policy), 1011 (unexpected)
- [ ] Stdin serialization: Mutex prevents interleaved writes
- [ ] --header applied to WS upgrade response
- [ ] EPIPE: close all clients with 1011
- [ ] Child exit: close all clients with 1011
- [ ] Client disconnect: remove from map, clean up multiplexer entries
- [ ] Health endpoint (HTTP, not WS)
- [ ] Integration test: two clients, send request, verify correct routing
- [ ] Integration test: notification broadcast to all clients
- [ ] Integration test: slow client disconnected after backpressure timeout

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings
notes:
CRITICAL: Broadcast Without Head-of-Line Blocking pattern:
1. Acquire read lock on client map
2. Clone all client channel senders into a local Vec
3. Drop the lock
4. Iterate Vec and send to each (try_send or per-client spawned forwarder)
NEVER hold RwLock guard across async sends ŌĆö causes deadlocks.

Backpressure: Per-client bounded channel (256 msgs). Blocked >30s ŌåÆ disconnect client.

Stdin write serialization: Multiple WS clients may send concurrently.
All writes to shared child stdin MUST go through a Mutex to prevent interleaved JSON lines.

title: US-014: HTTP Client Stack
description:
HTTP/1.1 client for connecting to remote MCP servers. Used by client modes (SSEŌåÆstdio, HTTPŌåÆstdio).

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync HTTP client (per US-000 findings), else `hyper` or minimal HTTP/1.1 client
- TLS via rustls (for HTTPS URLs)
- Reuses --header and --oauth2Bearer for outgoing requests

## File: src/client/http.rs

## Core API
```rust
struct HttpClient {
    headers: Vec<(String, String)>,  // from --header + --oauth2Bearer
    // TLS config if needed
}

impl HttpClient {
    async fn post(&self, url: &str, body: &[u8], content_type: &str)
        -> Result<HttpResponse, HttpClientError>;
    async fn get_stream(&self, url: &str, accept: &str)
        -> Result<HttpStreamResponse, HttpClientError>;
    async fn delete(&self, url: &str) -> Result<HttpResponse, HttpClientError>;
}

struct HttpResponse {
    status: u16,
    headers: HashMap<String, String>,
    content_type: String,              // IMPORTANT: exposed for caller branching
    body: Vec<u8>,
}
```

## TLS/HTTPS Support
- URLs starting with `https://` use TLS (rustls)
- URLs starting with `http://` use plain TCP
- Invalid URL scheme: error at startup

## Content-Type Detection
The HTTP client MUST expose response Content-Type to callers. This is critical for US-017 (HTTPŌåÆstdio) which branches on:
- `text/event-stream` ŌåÆ parse as SSE stream (reuse US-015 SSE parser)
- `application/json` ŌåÆ parse as single JSON-RPC response
Streamable HTTP servers return SSE streams for POST requests.

## Error Response Body Parsing
On non-2xx responses:
- If Content-Type is application/json: parse body as JSON-RPC error, propagate
- Otherwise: wrap status code + body text in HttpClientError

## Custom Headers
All headers from --header flag are added to every outgoing request.
If --oauth2Bearer is set, add `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
Fixes TS bug D-003 (only adds header when actually provided).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST with JSON body, custom headers, Content-Type
- [ ] GET streaming (for SSE endpoints) ŌĆö returns AsyncRead/stream
- [ ] DELETE with custom headers
- [ ] TLS/HTTPS support via rustls
- [ ] Content-Type exposed on response for caller branching
- [ ] Error response body parsing (JSON-RPC error extraction on non-2xx)
- [ ] --header applied to all outgoing requests
- [ ] --oauth2Bearer: Authorization header only when provided with value
- [ ] Unit tests: POST, GET stream, DELETE, custom headers, HTTPS URL
- [ ] Test: Content-Type correctly extracted from response

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings
notes:
CRITICAL: Response Content-Type detection required.
The HTTP client must expose response Content-Type to callers so US-017 can branch:
- "text/event-stream" ŌåÆ parse as SSE stream (multiple event: message\ndata: <json>)
- "application/json" ŌåÆ parse as single JSON-RPC response
This is needed because Streamable HTTP servers return SSE streams for request POSTs.
The streaming GET path (for SSE client in US-015) already returns text/event-stream.

title: US-015: SSE Client (EventSource)
description:
SSE client (EventSource) for connecting to remote SSE-based MCP servers.

## Tech Stack
- Built on HttpClient (US-014) GET stream
- SSE event parsing per W3C EventSource spec

## File: src/client/sse.rs

## SSE Event Parsing
Parse `text/event-stream` format:
- `event: <type>\n` (default: "message")
- `data: <payload>\n` (may span multiple lines, concatenated with \n)
- `id: <id>\n` (last event ID for reconnection)
- `: comment\n` (ignored, used for keepalive)
- Blank line terminates event

## Endpoint URL Discovery
The SSE server sends the POST endpoint URL in the first event:
- `event: endpoint\ndata: <url>\n\n`
- This URL is where the client should POST JSON-RPC messages
- May be relative (resolve against server base URL) or absolute
- Client must wait for endpoint event before sending any messages

## Reconnection
If SSE connection drops:
- Reconnect with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, max 30s)
- Include `Last-Event-ID` header if available
- Server may send `retry:` field to override reconnect interval
- Log reconnection attempts at warn level

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Endpoint URL discovery: capture endpoint event, resolve relative URLs
- [ ] Keepalive comment lines: silently consumed
- [ ] Reconnection: exponential backoff with Last-Event-ID
- [ ] retry: field respected
- [ ] Yield parsed events as async stream
- [ ] Integration test: connect to SSE server, parse events
- [ ] Test: multi-line data events concatenated correctly
- [ ] Test: endpoint URL discovery (relative and absolute)
- [ ] Test: reconnection with Last-Event-ID

- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings

title: US-016: Gateway ŌĆö SSE->stdio
description:
Gateway mode: SSEŌåÆstdio. Client mode that bridges stdin to remote SSE-based MCP server.

## Tech Stack
- SSE Client (US-015) for remote connection
- HttpClient (US-014) for POST requests
- Codec (US-004) for stdin/stdout line-delimited IO
- Logger (US-008), Signal handler (US-009)

## File: src/gateway/sse_to_stdio.rs

## Flow
1. Connect to remote SSE server (--sse URL)
2. Wait for endpoint URL from SSE stream (endpoint event)
3. Read JSON-RPC messages from stdin (line-delimited)
4. POST each message to the endpoint URL via HttpClient
5. Receive responses/notifications via SSE stream
6. Write responses to stdout (line-delimited)

## Init-Result-Capture Pattern (CRITICAL)
The TS code captures the init response as a side-effect of client.connect():
1. First stdin message arrives (initialize or other)
2. Create MCP client, override request() to intercept initialize call
3. Call client.connect(transport) ŌĆö auto-sends initialize + notifications/initialized
4. The interceptor captures the init response, writes it to stdout
5. After connect() resolves, enter normal message loop

In Rust, implement this as:
1. Connect SSE transport, receive endpoint URL
2. First stdin message must be initialize request
3. POST initialize to endpoint URL
4. Capture initialize response from SSE stream
5. Write initialize response to stdout
6. POST notifications/initialized to endpoint URL
7. Enter normal bidirectional message loop

Do NOT double-send initialize. The init is part of the connect handshake.

## SSE Reconnection Handling
If SSE connection drops during operation:
- Attempt reconnection (from US-015 reconnection logic)
- During reconnection: buffer stdin messages (up to 100 messages or 10s, whichever first)
- If reconnection succeeds: flush buffered messages
- If reconnection fails (30s total timeout): exit(1)
- Log all reconnection attempts at warn level

## Log Direction Labels
Use correct direction labels (fixes TS bug D-006):
- StdinŌåÆRemote: `"Stdin ŌåÆ SSE:"`
- RemoteŌåÆStdout: `"SSE ŌåÆ Stdout:"`

## Exit Behavior
- Remote transport close ŌåÆ exit(1) (for pm2/systemd restart)
- Remote transport error ŌåÆ log only, do NOT exit (B-004 in DIVERGENCES.md)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Connect to --sse URL, wait for endpoint event
- [ ] Read stdin line-by-line, POST to endpoint URL
- [ ] Receive SSE events, write to stdout
- [ ] Init-result-capture: capture initialize response during connect, write to stdout
- [ ] SSE reconnection: buffer messages, flush on reconnect, exit on failure
- [ ] Correct log direction labels (Stdin ŌåÆ SSE, SSE ŌåÆ Stdout)
- [ ] Remote close: exit(1). Remote error: log only
- [ ] Custom headers applied to outgoing requests
- [ ] Integration test: connect to mock SSE server, send request, receive response
- [ ] Test: init-result-capture works correctly (only one initialize sent)
- [ ] Test: reconnection buffers and flushes messages

- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings

Init-result-capture and exit behavior patterns are identical to US-016 (SSEŌåÆstdio).
Same --header / --oauth2Bearer header injection applies.

title: US-017: Gateway ŌĆö Streamable HTTP->stdio
description:
Gateway mode: Streamable HTTPŌåÆstdio. Client mode that bridges stdin to remote Streamable HTTP MCP server.

## Tech Stack
- HttpClient (US-014) for POST/GET/DELETE requests
- SSE Client (US-015) for parsing SSE stream responses
- Codec (US-004) for stdin/stdout line-delimited IO
- Logger (US-008), Signal handler (US-009)

## File: src/gateway/http_to_stdio.rs

## Flow
1. Read JSON-RPC messages from stdin (line-delimited)
2. POST each message to remote --streamableHttp URL
3. Branch on response Content-Type:
   - `text/event-stream`: parse as SSE stream, write each event's data to stdout
   - `application/json`: parse as single JSON-RPC response, write to stdout
4. Handle Mcp-Session-Id from server response headers

## Init-Result-Capture Pattern (same as US-016)
1. First stdin message must be initialize request
2. POST initialize to server URL
3. Capture initialize response (may be SSE stream or JSON)
4. Write initialize response to stdout
5. POST notifications/initialized
6. Enter normal bidirectional message loop

## Server-Initiated SSE Stream
After initialization, open a GET request to the server URL with:
- `Accept: text/event-stream`
- `Mcp-Session-Id` header (from initialize response)
This stream receives server-initiated notifications and requests.
Write each SSE event's data to stdout as a JSON-RPC message line.

## Content-Type Response Handling
POST responses from Streamable HTTP servers may be:
- `application/json` ŌåÆ single JSON-RPC response, write to stdout
- `text/event-stream` ŌåÆ SSE stream with multiple events, write each to stdout
Client MUST check Content-Type and branch accordingly (uses HttpClient Content-Type exposure from US-014).

## Log Direction Labels
Correct direction labels:
- StdinŌåÆRemote: `"Stdin ŌåÆ HTTP:"`
- RemoteŌåÆStdout: `"HTTP ŌåÆ Stdout:"`

## Exit Behavior
Same as US-016:
- Remote transport close ŌåÆ exit(1)
- Remote transport error ŌåÆ log only

title: US-003a: GatewayError Type and HTTP Response Mapping
description:
Shared error type hierarchy used by all gateways. Implements Asupersync IntoResponse trait to convert errors to correct HTTP status + JSON-RPC error body.

## File: src/error.rs (new file)

## Error Type
```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
enum GatewayError {
    // Transport-level errors (plain text response body)
    #[error("not acceptable: {0}")]
    NotAcceptable(String),                   // 406
    #[error("unsupported media type: {0}")]
    UnsupportedMediaType(String),            // 415
    #[error("session not found")]
    SessionNotFound,                         // 404
    #[error("conflict: {0}")]
    Conflict(String),                        // 409 (e.g., GET SSE stream already open)
    #[error("method not allowed")]
    MethodNotAllowed,                        // 405
    #[error("payload too large")]
    PayloadTooLarge,                         // 413
    #[error("service unavailable: {0}")]
    ServiceUnavailable(String),              // 503 (max sessions, not ready)

    // Application-level errors (JSON-RPC error response body)
    #[error("malformed JSON")]
    MalformedJson,                           // 400 + JSON-RPC -32700
    #[error("invalid request: {0}")]
    InvalidRequest(String),                  // 200 + JSON-RPC -32600
    #[error("session closing")]
    SessionClosing,                          // 503 + JSON-RPC -32000
    #[error("child process dead")]
    ChildDead,                               // 502 + JSON-RPC -32603
    #[error("auto-init timeout")]
    AutoInitTimeout,                         // 502 + JSON-RPC -32603
    #[error("internal error: {0}")]
    Internal(String),                        // 500 + JSON-RPC -32603

    // Wrapped errors
    #[error("child error: {0}")]
    Child(#[from] ChildError),               // maps to ChildDead behavior
    #[error("session error: {0}")]
    Session(SessionError),                   // maps contextually
    #[error("I/O error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),              // maps to Internal
}
```

Note: `ChildError` and `SessionError` are placeholder types defined in their respective modules (child.rs, session.rs). They'll be concrete types when those beads are implemented.

## Complete Error Mapping Table (from PRD)
| Condition | HTTP Status | JSON-RPC Code | Response Body |
|---|---|---|---|
| Malformed JSON body | 400 | -32700 | JSON-RPC error |
| Valid JSON, invalid JSON-RPC | 200 | -32600 | JSON-RPC error |
| Missing Accept headers (StreamableHTTP) | 406 | ŌĆö | Plain text |
| Wrong Content-Type (StreamableHTTP) | 415 | ŌĆö | Plain text |
| No session / invalid session (stateful POST) | 404 | ŌĆö | Plain text |
| Session closing/closed | 503 | -32000 | JSON-RPC error |
| Child process dead | 502 | -32603 | JSON-RPC error |
| Auto-init timeout | 502 | -32603 | JSON-RPC error |
| Internal error (stateless catch-all) | 500 | -32603 | JSON-RPC error |
| Message too large | 413 | ŌĆö | Plain text |
| Max sessions reached | 503 | ŌĆö | Plain text |
| GET/DELETE on stateless | 405 | ŌĆö | Plain text |
| GET SSE stream already open | 409 | ŌĆö | Plain text |
| Not ready (health) | 503 | ŌĆö | Plain text |
| CORS preflight | 204 | ŌĆö | Empty |
| Notification-only POST | 202 | ŌĆö | Empty |

## IntoResponse Implementation
GatewayError must implement Asupersync's web framework IntoResponse trait.

For errors WITH JSON-RPC codes, response body is:
```json
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","error":{"code":<code>,"message":"<message>"},"id":null}
```
Content-Type: application/json

For errors WITHOUT JSON-RPC codes (transport-level), response body is plain text error message.
Content-Type: text/plain

## Helper Constructors
```rust
impl GatewayError {
    fn invalid_session() -> Self
    fn child_dead() -> Self
    fn max_sessions() -> Self
    fn not_ready() -> Self
    fn malformed_json() -> Self
    fn missing_accept() -> Self
    fn wrong_content_type(expected: &str, got: &str) -> Self
    fn payload_too_large() -> Self
    fn method_not_allowed() -> Self
    fn sse_stream_conflict() -> Self
    fn auto_init_timeout() -> Self
    fn session_closing() -> Self
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GatewayError enum with all variants from the error mapping table
- [ ] IntoResponse trait implementation mapping each variant to correct HTTP status
- [ ] JSON-RPC error response body for variants that include JSON-RPC codes (Content-Type: application/json)
- [ ] Plain text response body for transport-level errors (Content-Type: text/plain)
- [ ] All helper constructors listed above
- [ ] Unit tests: each error variant ŌåÆ correct HTTP status + correct body format (JSON-RPC vs plain text)
- [ ] Test: JSON-RPC error body has correct structure (jsonrpc, error.code, error.message, id:null)

title: US-002: Project Scaffolding and CLI
description:
Create the Rust project structure with Cargo.toml, toolchain pin, and complete CLI argument parsing using clap derive macros.

## Tech Stack

## File Structure
```
Cargo.toml
rust-toolchain.toml
src/
  main.rs
  cli.rs
```

## CLI Flags (Complete)
| Flag | Type | Default | Scope |
|------|------|---------|-------|
| `--stdio <cmd>` | string | ŌĆö | Input |
| `--sse <url>` | string | ŌĆö | Input |
| `--streamableHttp <url>` | string | ŌĆö | Input |
| `--outputTransport` | choice | mode-dependent | Output: stdio|sse|ws|streamableHttp |
| `--port` | number | 8000 | Server modes |
| `--baseUrl` | string | '' | stdio->SSE |
| `--ssePath` | string | /sse | stdio->SSE |
| `--messagePath` | string | /message | stdio->SSE, stdio->WS |
| `--streamableHttpPath` | string | /mcp | stdio->StreamableHTTP |
| `--logLevel` | choice | info | All: debug|info|none |
| `--cors [origins]` | array | ŌĆö | Server modes |
| `--healthEndpoint [eps]` | array | [] | Server modes |
| `--header [headers]` | array | [] | All |
| `--oauth2Bearer <token>` | string | ŌĆö | All |
| `--stateful` | boolean | false | stdio->StreamableHTTP |
| `--sessionTimeout <ms>` | number | ŌĆö | Stateful HTTP only |
| `--protocolVersion` | string | 2024-11-05 | Stateless HTTP only |

## Default outputTransport Logic
- If `--sse` or `--streamableHttp` input: default to `stdio`
- If `--stdio` input: default to `sse`
- Use clap-parsed input mode to determine (cleaner than TS raw argv scan)

## Valid Transport Combinations
Only these inputŌåÆoutput pairs are valid:
| Input | Valid Outputs |
|-------|--------------|
| `--stdio` | sse, ws, streamableHttp |
| `--sse` | stdio |
| `--streamableHttp` | stdio |

Reject invalid combinations at CLI parse time with clear error message.

## --header Parsing Rules
Format: `--header "Name: Value"`. Split on first `:` only (value may contain `:`).
Trim whitespace from both name and value. Reject headers without `:` separator.
Multiple `--header` flags accumulate into a Vec.

## --cors Behavior
- `--cors` absent: CORS disabled entirely (no CORS headers on any response)
- `--cors` present with no values: CORS enabled with `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`
- `--cors origin1 origin2`: CORS enabled with specific origins (reflect Origin if in list)

## --healthEndpoint Behavior
- `--healthEndpoint` absent: no health endpoint
- `--healthEndpoint /healthz`: single health endpoint at /healthz
- `--healthEndpoint /healthz /ready`: multiple health endpoints (both paths serve health)

## --oauth2Bearer Behavior
Only add `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header if flag is actually provided with a non-empty value.
Fixes TS bug D-003 where yargs always includes the key, producing "Bearer undefined".

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cargo.toml with all dependencies, `rust-toolchain.toml` (pinned nightly)
- [ ] clap struct with ALL flags per CLI Flags table. Mode-dependent defaults for `--outputTransport`
- [ ] Mutual exclusivity: exactly one of `--stdio`/`--sse`/`--streamableHttp`
- [ ] Valid transport combinations enforced (see table above)
- [ ] Path flags validated to start with `/`
- [ ] `--sessionTimeout` validated positive
- [ ] `--header` parsing: split on first `:`, trim, reject malformed
- [ ] `--cors` three-state behavior (absent / present-no-values / with-origins)
- [ ] `--healthEndpoint` multi-value support
- [ ] `--oauth2Bearer` only adds header when actually provided with value
- [ ] `--help` output lists all flags with camelCase names and mode-dependent defaults
- [ ] Exit codes: 0 success, 1 error (match TS)

title: US-003: JSON-RPC 2.0 Types
description:
Implement JSON-RPC 2.0 message types with serde_json::RawValue for opaque pass-through. This is the foundational protocol layer used by all gateways.

## Tech Stack
- serde + serde_json with RawValue
- IMPORTANT: serde_json::RawValue is a DST (like str). MUST use Box<RawValue> for owned values

## File: src/jsonrpc.rs

## Core Type: RawMessage
A flat struct is the wire type ŌĆö NOT an enum. The same struct holds requests, responses, notifications, and errors. Classify via helper methods.

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct RawMessage {
    jsonrpc: String,                        // always "2.0"
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    id: Option<Box<RawValue>>,              // number, string, or null ŌĆö preserved exactly
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    method: Option<String>,                 // present for requests/notifications
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    params: Option<Box<RawValue>>,          // opaque
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    result: Option<Box<RawValue>>,          // opaque
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    error: Option<Box<RawValue>>,           // opaque (contains code, message, data)
}
```

IMPORTANT: Do NOT use `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`. JSON-RPC messages may contain extension fields. Unknown fields must be silently preserved via `#[serde(flatten)] extra: HashMap<String, RawValue>` or simply dropped (dropping is acceptable for a transparent proxy).

## id Field Semantics
- `id: None` (field absent) ŌåÆ notification (no response expected)
- `id: Some(RawValue("null"))` ŌåÆ valid JSON-RPC id of null (response expected, response id will also be null)
- `id: Some(RawValue("42"))` ŌåÆ numeric id
- `id: Some(RawValue("\"abc\""))` ŌåÆ string id
Must distinguish None (absent) from Some(null). Use `#[serde(default, deserialize_with = "deserialize_optional_raw")]` or similar.

## Classification Helpers
```rust
fn is_request(&self) -> bool       // has id + method
fn is_response(&self) -> bool      // has id + (result or error), no method
fn is_notification(&self) -> bool  // has method, no id (id field absent, NOT id:null)
fn is_initialize_request(&self) -> bool // method == "initialize" && is_request()
fn method_str(&self) -> Option<&str> // convenience accessor
```

## Error Response Construction
Helper to build JSON-RPC error responses:
```rust
fn error_response(id: Option<Box<RawValue>>, code: i32, message: &str) -> RawMessage
```
Used by GatewayError (US-003a) and auto-init (US-011) to construct error bodies.
If id is None (notification that errored), use `RawValue::from_string("null")` for the response id.

## Batch Support
Batch = JSON array at top level. Deserialization strategy:
1. Try `serde_json::from_str::<RawMessage>(line)` first (common case, single message)
2. If that fails with "expected struct", try `serde_json::from_str::<Vec<RawMessage>>(line)`
3. If both fail ŌåÆ malformed JSON (log + discard for stdio, 400 for HTTP)
Alternatively: peek first non-whitespace byte. `{` ŌåÆ single, `[` ŌåÆ batch. This avoids double-parse.
Forward batch to child as a single line (re-serialize Vec<RawMessage> to one JSON line).

## Protocol Transparency Rules
- Unknown methods: forward without interpretation
- Arbitrary params/result shapes: forward as-is
- Arbitrary error.data: forward as-is
- Batch JSON-RPC requests (JSON arrays): forward to child as single line
- JSON-RPC notifications (no id): forward without awaiting response
- Server-initiated requests: forward from child/remote to client without interpretation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `RawMessage` struct (NOT enum) using `Box<RawValue>` for opaque pass-through of params/result/error
- [ ] id field: correctly distinguishes absent (None) vs null (Some(null)) vs value (Some(42), Some("abc"))
- [ ] `is_request()`, `is_response()`, `is_notification()`, `is_initialize_request()` helpers
- [ ] `error_response(id, code, message)` constructor helper
- [ ] No `deny_unknown_fields` ŌĆö silently accept extension fields
- [ ] Batch support: detect and deserialize JSON arrays as `Vec<RawMessage>`
- [ ] Unknown methods: deserialize without rejection
- [ ] Round-trip tests: request, response, notification, error, batch, null id, string id, numeric id, unknown method, large params
- [ ] Test: absent id vs explicit null id are distinct (`is_notification()` returns true only for absent)
- [ ] Test: error_response() produces valid JSON-RPC error with correct structure

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings

title: US-004: Line-Delimited Stdio Codec
description:
Line-delimited codec for reading/writing JSON-RPC messages over stdin/stdout pipes.

## Tech Stack
- AsyncBufRead/AsyncWrite from asupersync (or std BufReader wrapper)
- serde_json for parsing

## File: src/codec.rs

## Read Path
1. Read lines from AsyncBufRead (newline-delimited)
2. Line separator: accept BOTH \n AND \r\n (Windows compat). Split on \n, strip trailing \r
3. Skip empty lines (whitespace-only)
4. Per-message size limit: 16MB. If a single line exceeds 16MB, log error and skip (do not crash)
5. Parse each line as JSON via serde_json::from_str::<RawMessage>
6. On parse failure: log at error level with first 200 chars of line, skip message
7. Partial line buffer limit: 64MB max (from PRD). If partial buffer exceeds 64MB, treat as protocol violation ŌĆö kill child/session

## Write Path
1. Serialize RawMessage to JSON string (serde_json::to_string)
2. Append \n
3. Write to AsyncWrite
4. Flush after each message (critical for pipe buffering ŌĆö child needs messages immediately)

## Global Memory Budget
The 64MB partial buffer limit is per-child. There is a 256MB global cap across all sessions (see US-005 notes). The codec itself enforces the per-child 64MB limit. The global semaphore is owned by ChildBridge (US-005) and passed in at construction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `read_message()`: read one line, parse as RawMessage, return
- [ ] `write_message()`: serialize + newline + flush
- [ ] Line separator: handles both \n and \r\n (strip \r before JSON parse)
- [ ] Empty lines skipped
- [ ] Per-message size limit: 16MB max per line. Lines exceeding limit are logged and skipped
- [ ] Partial buffer limit: 64MB max. Exceeded ŌåÆ return fatal error (caller kills child)
- [ ] Parse errors: logged at error level with truncated preview, message skipped
- [ ] Batch support: JSON array lines forwarded as-is (parsed as Vec<RawMessage> by caller, not codec)
- [ ] Unit tests: single message, batch, empty lines, \r\n, oversized message, parse error, partial buffer overflow
- [ ] Benchmark: 10k messages round-trip under 100ms

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings
notes:
IMPORTANT: Line separator must accept BOTH 
 AND 
 for Windows compatibility.
Split on 
, then strip trailing  from each line before JSON parsing.

title: US-005: Child Process Manager (ChildBridge)
description:
ChildBridge: spawn child MCP servers via `sh -c`, manage process groups with setsid/killpg, handle stdin/stdout/stderr as separate async tasks.

## Tech Stack
- Use `asupersync::process::Command` if available (per US-000 findings), else `std::process::Command` with async wrapper
- `nix` crate for setsid/killpg/waitpid
- Spawned inside Asupersync Region ŌĆö Region cancel kills child

## File: src/child.rs

## Spawn Sequence
1. `Command::new("sh").arg("-c").arg(stdio_cmd)` with `pre_exec(|| { setsid(); Ok(()) })`
2. `stdin(Stdio::piped())`, `stdout(Stdio::piped())`, `stderr(Stdio::piped())`
3. `.take()` stdin, stdout, stderr into separate async tasks

## Stdout Channel Design (CRITICAL for deadlock prevention)
Stdout reader task writes to a bounded async channel (capacity 256 messages).
If channel is full (child producing faster than gateway consuming):
1. Log warning at debug level
2. Block the stdout reader task (natural backpressure to child via pipe buffer)
3. Do NOT drop messages
This prevents unbounded memory growth while keeping the child alive.
Gateway consumer tasks read from the receiver end. On child exit, stdout reader closes the sender.

## Stdout Invalid UTF-8
Child stdout may contain invalid UTF-8. Use lossy decode (`String::from_utf8_lossy`).
Do NOT crash or skip messages ŌĆö pass the lossy-decoded string to JSON parsing.
If JSON parsing fails (invalid JSON, not just invalid UTF-8), log at error level and skip.

## Kill Sequence
1. SIGTERM to process group (`killpg(pgid, SIGTERM)`)
2. Wait up to 5s for child exit
3. Check `waitpid(child_pid, WNOHANG)` before SIGKILL (PID reuse safety)
4. If still alive: SIGKILL to process group
5. Drain remaining stdout (up to 1s drain budget after kill, then drop)
6. Close all FDs

## Shutdown Drain Budget
On graceful shutdown (Region cancel or signal):
1. Send SIGTERM to process group
2. Allow up to 1s for remaining stdout messages to drain through channel
3. After 1s, close channel receiver (messages lost ŌĆö acceptable on shutdown)
4. SIGKILL if child hasn't exited within 5s total

## EPIPE Handling
- On broken stdin pipe: mark child as dead immediately, stop accepting new messages
- Propagate error to caller with transport-appropriate status (HTTP 502, WS close, SSE error event)
- Stateful sessions with dead child are terminally failed (no auto-restart ŌĆö MCP session state lost)

## Stderr Handling
- Captured and logged at error level (all modes consistently)
- Buffer limit: 64KB per read. Lines >4KB truncated with `[truncated]` suffix
- Never queued ŌĆö logged inline by stderr reader task
- Invalid UTF-8: lossy decode (replace with U+FFFD), do not crash

## Grandchildren
- `setsid` creates new process group. `killpg` sends signal to entire group
- Handles TS bug where `child.kill()` only kills `sh`, leaving `npm`/`node` orphaned

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spawn via `sh -c` with `pre_exec(setsid)` for process group
- [ ] Use asupersync process Command if available, else std::process::Command with async wrapper
- [ ] Stdin/stdout `.take()`-ed into separate async tasks
- [ ] Stdout reader writes to bounded channel (256 capacity) ŌĆö blocks on full, does not drop
- [ ] Stdout invalid UTF-8: lossy decode, pass to JSON parser, log+skip on parse failure
- [ ] `kill()`: SIGTERM to pgid -> 5s wait -> SIGKILL (with PID reuse safety check via waitpid WNOHANG)
- [ ] `Drop` impl sends SIGTERM to pgid (safety net)
- [ ] EPIPE detection: on broken stdin pipe, mark child dead, propagate error to caller
- [ ] Child exit detection: detect via wait status, propagate to owning session/gateway
- [ ] Stderr: read + log at error level, 64KB buffer, 4KB line truncation, lossy UTF-8 decode
- [ ] Spawned inside Region ŌĆö Region cancel kills child
- [ ] Shutdown drain: 1s drain budget after SIGTERM, then close channel
- [ ] Integration test: spawn, read message, cancel Region, verify pgid killed within 2s
- [ ] Integration test: write to dead child stdin, verify EPIPE handling
- [ ] Integration test: rapid spawn/kill cycles (100+), verify no leaked pids or fds

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings
notes:
IMPORTANT: Global memory semaphore ŌĆö 64MB max partial buffer per child, 256MB global cap across ALL sessions.
Implement as Arc<Semaphore> passed to each ChildBridge on spawn. New sessions rejected (503) when budget exhausted.
This prevents worst case 64MB ├Ś 1024 sessions = 64GB memory exhaustion.

PID 1 zombie reaping: NOT owned by this bead. Zombie reaping (SIGCHLD handler with waitpid(-1, WNOHANG)) is owned by US-009 (Signal Handling). ChildBridge only reaps its OWN child via direct waitpid(child_pid).

title: US-006: Session Manager
description:
Session manager for stateful Streamable HTTP mode. Manages session lifecycle, access counting, and timeout.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync Region per session (Region cancel cascades to ChildBridge cleanup)
- DashMap or RwLock<HashMap> for session storage
- UUID v4 for session IDs (Mcp-Session-Id header)

## File: src/session.rs

## Session State Machine
```
Created ŌåÆ Active ŌåÆ Closing ŌåÆ Closed
                     Ōåæ
              (DELETE or timeout)
```
- Created: session exists, child spawning
- Active: child alive, accepting requests
- Closing: received DELETE or timeout fired. 5s drain timeout for in-flight requests, then Closed
- Closed: session removed from map, Region cancelled, child killed

## ClosingŌåÆClosed Drain Timeout
When entering Closing state:
1. Stop accepting NEW requests (return 404 for this session ID)
2. Allow in-flight requests up to 5s to complete
3. After 5s or all in-flight requests complete (whichever first), transition to Closed
4. Closed: cancel Region, kill child, remove from session map

## Access Counting (RAII Guard)
```rust
struct SessionAccessGuard { session_id: String, counter: Arc<AtomicU64> }
impl Drop for SessionAccessGuard { fn drop(&mut self) { self.counter.fetch_sub(1, Relaxed); } }
```
- Increment on request accept, decrement on response terminal state (via Drop)
- Timeout only fires when count reaches zero (no in-flight requests)
- Must be cancellation-safe ŌĆö Drop always runs, even on task cancel

## Cleanup Callback
Session manager accepts a cleanup callback for when sessions are removed:
```rust
type CleanupFn = Box<dyn Fn(SessionId) + Send + Sync>;
```
This allows the gateway to clean up per-session resources (e.g., SSE streams, child processes).

## Global Memory Semaphore
256MB global memory budget shared across all sessions (see US-005). Session creation checks semaphore:
- If budget available: create session, acquire semaphore permit
- If budget exhausted: reject with 503 (max sessions / resource exhaustion)
The semaphore is an Arc<Semaphore> passed to SessionManager at construction.

## Max Sessions
Default: 1024 concurrent sessions. Enforced in session creation. Return 503 when limit reached.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session CRUD: create (with child spawn), get, delete
- [ ] State machine: Created ŌåÆ Active ŌåÆ Closing ŌåÆ Closed
- [ ] Closing state: 5s drain timeout for in-flight requests, then Closed
- [ ] Access counting with RAII guard (Drop-based decrement, cancellation-safe)
- [ ] Session timeout: configurable via --sessionTimeout flag, only fires when access count = 0
- [ ] DELETE handler: transition to Closing, return 202
- [ ] Max sessions (1024 default): return 503 when limit reached
- [ ] Global memory semaphore integration: check on session creation, reject if exhausted
- [ ] Cleanup callback: invoked when session transitions to Closed
- [ ] Session stored inside Asupersync Region ŌĆö Region cancel kills child + cleans up
- [ ] Unit tests: create/get/delete, timeout, concurrent access, max sessions, access guard drop on cancel

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings

title: US-007: CORS and Headers
description:
CORS middleware and custom header injection for all server modes.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync middleware/layer pattern (per US-000 findings)
- Applied to all HTTP routes in server modes

## File: src/cors.rs

## CORS Behavior (Three States)
1. `--cors` absent: No CORS headers at all. No OPTIONS handling.
2. `--cors` (present, no values): `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` on all responses. Handle OPTIONS preflight with 204.
3. `--cors origin1 origin2`: Reflect `Origin` header if it's in the allowed list. Add `Vary: Origin` header. Handle OPTIONS preflight with 204.

## CorsConfig
```rust
enum CorsConfig {
    Disabled,                    // --cors absent
    AllowAll,                    // --cors with no values
    AllowList(Vec<String>),      // --cors origin1 origin2
}
```

## Preflight Response (OPTIONS)
- Status: 204 No Content
- `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`: * or reflected origin
- `Access-Control-Allow-Methods`: GET, POST, DELETE, OPTIONS
- `Access-Control-Allow-Headers`: Content-Type, Accept, Mcp-Session-Id, Last-Event-ID
- `Access-Control-Expose-Headers`: Mcp-Session-Id (when stateful HTTP mode ŌĆö set via `expose_session_header: bool`)
- `Vary: Origin` (when using AllowList mode)

## Custom Headers (--header)
- Applied to all responses as additional headers
- Injected after CORS headers (so custom headers can override CORS if desired)
- Format already parsed by CLI (US-002): Vec<(String, String)>

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CorsConfig enum with three states (Disabled, AllowAll, AllowList)
- [ ] CORS disabled: no CORS headers, no OPTIONS handling
- [ ] CORS AllowAll: `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` on all responses
- [ ] CORS AllowList: reflect Origin if in list, add `Vary: Origin`
- [ ] OPTIONS preflight: 204 with correct Allow-Methods, Allow-Headers, Expose-Headers
- [ ] Expose-Headers includes Mcp-Session-Id when `expose_session_header` is true
- [ ] Custom --header values applied to all responses
- [ ] Middleware composable with Asupersync's web framework
- [ ] Unit tests: CORS disabled, wildcard, origin list, OPTIONS preflight, custom headers, Vary: Origin

## Quality Gates

- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings

title: US-008: Logging and Observability
description:
Logging infrastructure and observability counters for all modes.

## Tech Stack
- Custom Logger struct wrapping stderr/stdout routing
- AtomicU64 counters for all metrics
- No external logging framework (keep deps minimal)

## Files: src/observe.rs (counters), src/logger.rs (logging)

## Log Routing
- When outputTransport is `stdio`: ALL logs go to stderr (stdout is the MCP transport)
- Otherwise: info to stdout, error to stderr
- Wrap in a Logger struct that routes based on configured output transport

## Log Levels
- `debug`: verbose protocol messages, counter snapshots
- `info`: startup, connections, mode transitions
- `none`: suppress all output
- Format: `[LEVEL] message` (no timestamps ŌĆö pm2/systemd add those)

## Startup Message Format
On startup, log at info level:
```
supergateway v<version> | <inputMode> ŌåÆ <outputMode> | port <port>
```
Example: `supergateway v0.1.0 | stdio ŌåÆ streamableHttp | port 8000`

## Counter API
```rust
pub struct Metrics {
    pub active_sessions: AtomicU64,
    pub active_children: AtomicU64,
    pub active_clients: AtomicU64,
    pub total_requests: AtomicU64,
    pub total_spawns: AtomicU64,
    pub spawn_failures: AtomicU64,
    pub session_timeouts: AtomicU64,
    pub forced_kills: AtomicU64,
    pub client_disconnects: AtomicU64,
    pub backpressure_events: AtomicU64,
    pub queue_depth_max: AtomicU64,
    pub decode_errors: AtomicU64,
}

impl Metrics {
    pub fn inc(&self, counter: &AtomicU64) { counter.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed); }
    pub fn dec(&self, gauge: &AtomicU64) { gauge.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::Relaxed); }
    pub fn snapshot(&self) -> MetricsSnapshot { /* read all fields into plain struct */ }
}
```
Shared as `Arc<Metrics>` across all gateway tasks.

## "Logged on Change" Semantics
Gauge counters (active_sessions, active_children, active_clients) are logged at debug level when they change:
- Log the new value after increment/decrement
- Use `inc_and_log()` / `dec_and_log()` wrappers that combine the atomic op with a debug log
- This avoids periodic polling ŌĆö changes are logged inline

## Health Detail Endpoint
When `--logLevel debug` and `?detail=true` query param:
- Call `metrics.snapshot()` and serialize to JSON
- Return as health response body with Content-Type: application/json
- When not debug or no ?detail: return plain "ok" text

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Logger struct with stderr/stdout routing based on output transport
- [ ] Log levels: debug, info, none. Format: `[LEVEL] message`
- [ ] Startup message: `supergateway v<version> | <input> ŌåÆ <output> | port <port>`
- [ ] Metrics struct with all counters listed above as AtomicU64
- [ ] `inc()`, `dec()`, `snapshot()` methods
- [ ] `inc_and_log()` / `dec_and_log()` wrappers that log gauge changes at debug level
- [ ] Arc<Metrics> shared across tasks
- [ ] Health detail endpoint: JSON metrics snapshot when debug + ?detail=true
- [ ] Test: logger routes to stderr when output=stdio
- [ ] Test: metrics increment/decrement/snapshot
- [ ] Test: health detail returns JSON with all counter fields

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` ŌĆö no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` ŌĆö all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` ŌĆö no warnings
notes:
Counter implementation pattern:
```rust
pub struct Metrics {
    pub active_sessions: AtomicU64,
    pub active_children: AtomicU64,
    pub active_clients: AtomicU64,
    pub total_requests: AtomicU64,
    pub total_spawns: AtomicU64,
    pub spawn_failures: AtomicU64,
    pub session_timeouts: AtomicU64,
    pub forced_kills: AtomicU64,
    pub client_disconnects: AtomicU64,
    pub backpressure_events: AtomicU64,
    pub queue_depth_max: AtomicU64,
    pub decode_errors: AtomicU64,
}

impl Metrics {
    pub fn inc(&self, counter: &AtomicU64) { counter.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed); }
    pub fn dec(&self, gauge: &AtomicU64) { gauge.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::Relaxed); }
    pub fn snapshot(&self) -> MetricsSnapshot { /* read all fields */ }
}
```
Shared as Arc<Metrics> across all gateway tasks. All gateways call these methods.
Health detail endpoint calls snapshot() and serializes to JSON.

Log routing: Use eprintln! for all log output when outputTransport is stdio.
Use println! for info, eprintln! for error otherwise. Wrap in a Logger struct.
